name: Dogs
description: Breeds, famous pups, and all things canine

questions.q: Which breed is often dubbed the smartest dog, famous for its herding ability?

source.notes: Originally from the Anglo-Scottish border. Chaser the Border Collie learned 1,022 words.

questions.q: What breed is famously known as the Queen of England's (Elizabeth II) favorite?

source.notes: Elizabeth II owned 30+ Pembroke Welsh Corgis over 70 years.

questions.q: Which breed is known as the world's fastest dog, capable of over 40 mph?

source.notes: Up to 45 mph. Used in racing and as family pets.

questions.q: Which Alpine rescue dog is famous for carrying brandy around its neck?

source.notes: Bred by Swiss monks for Alpine rescue. The brandy cask is a myth but iconic.

questions.q: What is another name for a German Shepherd?

source.notes: Called 'Alsatian' in the UK after WWI to avoid anti-German sentiment.

questions.q: The Borzoi is a sighthound originally bred in which country for hunting wolves?

source.notes: Name means 'swift' in Russian. Favored by the Russian aristocracy.

questions.q: What is the smallest breed of dog in the world, by AKC standards?

source.notes: Named after the Mexican state of Chihuahua. Weigh as little as 2 lbs.

questions.q: Which dog breed's name comes from the French word for 'butterfly,' referring to their ear shape?

source.notes: 'Papillon' = butterfly in French. Referring to the butterfly-winged ears.

questions.q: Which breed's tall, muscular profile earned it the nickname 'Apollo of Dogs'?

source.notes: Can reach 32+ inches at the shoulder. Despite the name, Great Danes originate from Germany.

questions.q: Which of these breeds has Asian origins and a curled tail?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: reworded stem (these aren't strictly named after a country). Akita and Shiba Inu are Japanese; Chow Chow is Chinese; all have curled tails.

questions.q: What does 'Rottweiler' take its name from?

source.notes: From Rottweil, Germany. Originally bred as cattle herders and butcher's dogs.

questions.q: What is a dog with no specific breed (mixed heritage) commonly called?

source.notes: 'Designer dog' specifically refers to intentional crosses like Labradoodles.

questions.q: Which breed is commonly associated with guarding Tibetan monasteries?

source.notes: Ancient guard dog breed. Can weigh over 150 lbs.

questions.q: Which short-legged breed resembles a hot dog and is nicknamed 'wiener dog'?

source.notes: 'Dachshund' = 'badger dog' in German. Bred to hunt badgers from their dens.

questions.q: The Siberian Husky was originally bred by which indigenous people?

source.notes: Native Siberian people who used the dogs for sled pulling in extreme cold.

questions.q: Which breed has skin with deep wrinkles and a 'hippopotamus' appearance?

source.notes: Shar Pei means 'sand skin' in Chinese, referring to the rough coat.

questions.q: What is the most popular dog breed in the United States (AKC registrations)?

source.notes: French Bulldog overtook Labrador in 2022 after 31 years of Lab dominance.

questions.q: Which dog breed is known for its distinctive spotted coat?

source.notes: Born pure white; spots develop over 3-4 weeks. Historic coaching dog.

questions.q: The Poodle, despite its association with France, was originally bred in which country?

source.notes: Germanic origins as a water retriever. 'Pudel' comes from German 'puddeln' (to splash).

questions.q: Approximately how many dog breeds are recognized by the American Kennel Club (AKC)?

source.notes: ~200 fully recognized breeds as of 2024. FCI (international) recognizes 356.
